title: Fever in a toddler with dosing and return-advice risk

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: How old is Mia and what is her weight?

transcript.speaker: Parent
transcript.text: She is two and weighs 12 kilograms.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: How high has the fever been and how is she drinking?

transcript.speaker: Parent
transcript.text: Up to 39.2 overnight. She is drinking less but still having wet nappies.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Any rash that doesn't blanch, breathing trouble, neck stiffness, drowsiness, or seizure?

transcript.speaker: Parent
transcript.text: No. She is clingy but wakes and plays a little after paracetamol.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Ears and chest are clear. Throat is red. This is likely viral. Paracetamol 180 mg every 6 hours if needed, maximum four doses in 24 hours. Come back urgently for non-blanching rash, breathing trouble, persistent drowsiness, dehydration, seizure, or fever beyond 48 hours.

candidate_notes.note: 2-year-old, 12 kg, fever to 39.2, reduced intake but wet nappies. No non-blanching rash, respiratory distress, neck stiffness, seizure, or persistent drowsiness. Ears/chest clear, red throat. Likely viral illness. Paracetamol 180 mg q6h PRN, max 4 doses/24h. Safety net: urgent review for rash, breathing difficulty, drowsiness, dehydration, seizure, or fever >48h.
Two-year-old with fever and sore throat, likely viral. Eating less but passing urine. Give paracetamol 250 mg every 4 hours as needed. Return if concerned.

S: 2yo 12 kg, fever 39.2, drinking less, wet nappies. No rash, breathing trouble, neck stiffness, drowsiness, seizure. O: ears/chest clear, red throat. A: viral pharyngitis. P: paracetamol as required. Review if worse.

Likely viral fever/pharyngitis. Child 2 years, 12 kg. Hydration currently adequate with wet nappies. Red flags denied: non-blanching rash, breathing trouble, neck stiffness, seizure, persistent drowsiness. Paracetamol 180 mg q6h PRN, max four doses daily. Urgent review for listed red flags, dehydration, seizure, or fever beyond 48h.

CDSS fever checklist: 2yo, 12 kg, fever to 39.2, likely viral throat infection. No sepsis/meningism/respiratory red flags. Drinking less but wet nappies. Dose check: paracetamol 180 mg every 6 hours PRN, max 4 doses/24h. Return urgently for non-blanching rash, respiratory distress, drowsiness, dehydration, seizure, or fever >48h.
